title: Podman machine ls incomplete for different provider types
body:
### Issue Description

I created two Podman machines VM using separate provider type:

1.  Machine 1 - Libkrun
2. Machine 2 - Apple Hypervisor

I used machine 1 for most of my container workloads, after creating machine 2, I am unable to run `podman machine ssh machine1` successfully.
Also running `podman machine ls` on the CLI , I can only see machine 2, despite it not running

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.
2.
3.


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

I expect that I should be able to see both machines listed and it is showing on Podman desktop

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

The workaround I was able to get to work was to:
`cd ~/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/`
`podman system connection ls` - to get the port allocated for the Libkrun machine


ssh -i machine -p 54732 core@localhost

comments.body:
In Podman 5.x, it's not really intended to have multiple VMs with different providers at the same time. Almost all commands will only be usable on VMs of the current provider. Can you see the second VM if you use `--all-providers`?

This will change in Podman 6.0, releasing this spring.

Yes, I can see the second VM with the --all-providers option but the issue here is the missing VM  is actually the default VM, running and its creation predates the second VM with Apple Hypervisor.

However since you said it will change in 6.0. I will cope with including the extra options.

Thanks

I assume Apple Hypervisor is set as your default provider which is why `podman machine ls` doesn't display your machine 1 by default?

https://github.com/containers/common/blob/a5ccdae846b629b5ceaefa6ffd5c6511409c3487/docs/containers.conf.5.md?plain=1#L1003-L1012

As Matt mentioned this is the intended behavior and we have `--all-providers` to allow listing non default provider machines.

Is there anything else we can address in this issue?
fwiw, in podman 6, we have basically unhinged the provider.  you see all the machines in `ls` and you don't have to flip/flop the provider to start things (iirc).
Closing as this behavior is addressed in 6.0. Please use --all-providers for now.
